Chapter - 10 The Manners Of Going To The Bathroom 245 Abu Hurairah later related, ‘Then I said, ‘What is the problem with bones and manure?’ He ## said,

‘They are the food of Jinns.’ A delegation of Jinns from Naseebeen - and good indeed are those Jinns - came to me and asked me for provision, and so I invoked Allah 8% for them - that they should never pass by any bones or manure except that they should find food upon them.”“) This

narration makes clear the reason why it is forbidden to purify oneself with either bones or manure.

Related Issue: Based on the above-mentioned ruling for the food of Jinn, it is also forbidden to perform Al-Istinjaa or Al-Istijmaar with the food of human beings. Similarly, it is forbidden to perform Al-Istinjaa or Al-Istijmaar with honored paper, such as paper on which religious knowledge is written, for that paper is likely to have written on it verses of the Quran or Allah’s beautiful Names and Attributes. And an even worst offence is to use the Qur’an itself.

11) When One Performs Al-Istijmaar, It ls Recommended To Clean An Odd Number Of Times And To Use An Odd Number Of Stones

One should purify himself no less than three times, for in the previously related hadith of Salmaan , the Prophet #¥ said,

“Let no one from you purify himself with less than three stones.” Even if full purification is achieved with less than three wipes, one must wipe again to achieve the minimum amount. And if ull purification is achieved after the third wiping, and if it is ichieved at an even number - such as four or six - then it is ‘ecommended to wipe one more time, in order to finish at an odd

wmber. In a hadith related by Abu Hurairah , the Prophet # aid,

"| Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Manaaqib (3860).

“When one of you performs Al-Istijmaar, then let him perform it an odd number of times...”""1

12) When One Is In The Bathroom, It Is Disliked For Him To Talk

") Related by Bukhaaree (161), Muslim (237), Ahmad (7180), An-Nassaaee (88), Abu Daawood (35), Ibn Maajah (409), Maalik (34), and Ad-Daarimee (703). And the above-mentioned wording is from Muslim’s narration of the hadith.

Chapter - 11 The Manners Of Attending Masjids 247

The Manners Of Attending Masjids

“O Children of Adam! Take your adornment (by wearing your clean clothes), while praying and going round (the Tawaf of) the Ka‘bah.” (Quran 7:31)

The Prophet #% said, shal ¢ Zk alan Jy hs te ib J Ol fb tll SG

“Whoever performs ablution (Wudoo) for prayer, performs it well, then walks to the prescribed prayer, and then prays it with the people, or with the congregation, or in the Masjid - Allah forgives for him his sins.”"

The Etiquettes Of Attending The Masjid

1) The Prohibition Of Going To The Masjid After Eating [Raw] Garlic, Onion, Or Anything Else That Emits A Foul Smell

When a person eats raw onion or garlic, he should abstain from going to the Masjid, so as to avoid inflicting harm upon other worshippers with his foul smell; and whosoever harms worshippers harms the angels as well. Jaabir 4 reported that the Prophet # said,

" Related by Muslim (232).

“Whoever eats garlic or onion, then let him withdraw from us,” or he #8 said, ‘Then let him stay away from our Masjid, and let him sit in his home.’

In another hadith, Jaabir # said, “The Messenger of Allah # forbade us from eating onion and leek, but we became overcome by need, and so we ate from them. Then he # said,

“Whoever eats from this foul-smelling tree, then let him not come near our Masjid, for whatever man is harmed by, angels are harmed by as well."

Even though these Ahadeeth expressly prohibit Muslims from going to the Masjid after eating garlic and onion, and even though they are absolved of sin for not attending congregational prayer, some people still violate the prohibition. Allah 4¢ said in His Book:

“And let those who oppose the Messenger’s (Muhammad 3%) commandment (i.e., his Sunnah - legal ways, orders, acts of worship, statements, etc.) beware, lest some Fitnah (disbelief, trials, afflictions, earthquakes, killing, overpowered by a tyrant,

etc.) befall them or a painful torment be inflicted on them.”

True, some of them do not intentionally oppose the Messenger’s commandment; they simply don’t want to miss the congregational prayer, yet that is not an acceptable excuse. Some Muslims from among the masses know about the prohibition but pay no heed to it, an act of negligence that stems from weak Eemaan in their hearts.

i Related by Bukhaaree (855).

'2] Related by Bukhaaree (854), Muslim (564), Ahmad (14596), An-Nassaaee (707), At-Tirmidhee (1806), and Abu Daawood (3823). And the abovementioned wording is from Muslim’s narration of the hadith,

Chapter - 11 The Manners Of Attending Masjids 249

Related Issue: The ruling for garlic, onion, and leek applies to anything that has a foul odor and that will inflict harm upon worshippers - such as cigarette smoke or foul odors that emanate from a person’s body or clothes. A worshipper should consider his situation before he goes to the Masjid, so that he doesn’t end up sinning as a result of inflicting harm on Muslims.

If you eat garlic and onion and then use something to suppress their foul odor, you may go to the Masjid, but you must first be sure that the smell is completely suppressed and that the smell coming from your mouth will not bother other worshippers. Some people make the mistake of using toothpaste to get rid of the smell of garlic and onion, but they are wrong, for the smell of garlic and onion originates from the stomach and not from the mouth.

2) It Is Recommended To Go To The Masjid Early

The Prophet #% exhorted Muslims to go to the Masjid early. Abu Hurairah # related that the Messenger of Allah # said,

“Had people known what is in the Nidaa (the calling to the prayer, ie., the Adhaan) and the first row, and had they found no way (to be in the first row and to perform the call to prayer) except by casting lots, they would have cast lots (so determined would they be). And had people known what is in (i.e., what the virtues are) being early (to the Masjid for prayer), they would have raced to it. And had they known what is in the [congregational] ‘Eesha and

Morning (Fajr) prayers, they would have come to them, even if they had to crawl in order to get there,"]

Muslim narrated it thus: ‘Had you known or had they known

{1 Related by Bukhaaree (615), Muslim (437), Ahmad (7185), At-Tirmidhee (225), An-Nassaaee (540), and Maalik (151).

what is in the front row, [seeing who would be in that row] would have had to be settled by casting lots (because everyone would race to be in that row). These narrations clearly show the virtue and superiority and great reward of going early to the Masjid. That the Prophet 2% did not specify the reward for going to the Masjid early, but instead informed us what lengths we would go to had we known the exact reward, proves that it is indeed a great reward.

3) Walking To The Masjid With Calmness And Serenity

When you walk to the Masjid for prayer, you should not walk as if you are rushed or agitated; instead, you should walk with a sense of calmness and peace. As a result, you are more likely to feel peaceful and concentrated in your prayer. But if you come to prayer in a hurry, by the time you enter into its state, your mind is likely to be distracted and unfocused. The Prophet # forbade the people of his Nation from running and rushing to the prayer, even if the prayer is in progress. Abu Qataadah 4 said, ‘While we were praying with the Prophet 2%, he #% heard the clamor of men. When he finished praying, he # said,

oe SAS tan ‘What is the matter with you?’ They said, ‘We hurried to [join the] prayer.’ He # said, “Do not do so. When you come to prayer, adhere to As-Sakeenah (calmness and quietness). Whatever [part of the prayer] you get,

then pray [it], and whatever you miss, complete it (after the Imam makes Tasleem).’"?!

] Related by Bukhaaree (615), Muslim (437, 439), Ahmad (7680), AtTirmidhee (225), and An-Nassaaee (540).

Chapter - 11 The Manners Of Attending Masjids 251

And Abu Hurairah 4 reported that he heard the Messenger of Allah # say, “When the prayer Ugeemat (when the Iqaamah is made and the prayer commences), then do not come racing (running or jogging) to it, but instead come to it, walking, with a sense of As-Sakeenah (peace and quite) about you. Whatever you get, then pray it; and whatever you miss, then complete it (after the Imam makes Tasleem).”")

One who reflects on both Ahadeeth might notice a difference between them: Abu Qataadah’s narration has the wording, “When you come to prayer,” while Abu Hurairah’s narration has the wording, “When the prayer Ugeemat (when the Iqaamah is made and the prayer commences).” Is there a difference in meaning between the two wordings? According to Abu Qataadah’s narration, when a worshipper goes to the Masjid, he must go with a sense of calm and quiet about him, regardless of whether the prayer has commenced or not. Abu Hurairah’s narration, on the other hand, points to the main reason why people run to the Masjid: the prayer is in progress, and they don’t want to miss any of it. That is the combined meaning of both wordings, and Allah 3 knows best.

4) What Supplication You Should Say While You Are Walking To The Masjid For prayer

When you walk to the Masjid, it is recommended for you to say

what the Prophet #% said when he would go out for prayer. Ibn

‘Abbaas 4 related that the Prophet #%, ‘Then went out for

prayer, and he # was saying,
content_id:
Bab - 10 Adab Masuk Kamar Mandi 245 Abu Hurairah kemudian meriwayatkan, 'Kemudian aku berkata, 'Apa masalahnya dengan tulang dan kotoran?' Beliau ## bersabda,

'Keduanya adalah makanan jin.' Sebuah delegasi jin dari Naseebeen - dan memang baik jin-jin tersebut - datang kepadaku dan meminta bekal, maka aku pun berdoa kepada Allah 8% untuk mereka - agar mereka tidak pernah melewati tulang atau kotoran apapun kecuali mereka akan mendapati makanan padanya."") Riwayat ini

menjelaskan alasan mengapa dilarang bersuci dengan tulang atau kotoran.

Masalah Terkait: Berdasarkan hukum yang telah disebutkan mengenai makanan jin, maka juga diharamkan melakukan Al-Istinjaa atau Al-Istijmaar dengan makanan manusia. Demikian pula, diharamkan melakukan Al-Istinjaa atau Al-Istijmaar dengan kertas yang dimuliakan, seperti kertas yang ditulis padanya ilmu agama, karena kertas tersebut kemungkinan besar memuat ayat-ayat Quran atau Nama-nama dan Sifat-sifat Allah yang indah. Dan yang lebih buruk lagi adalah menggunakan Quran itu sendiri.

11) Ketika Melakukan Al-Istijmaar, Dianjurkan Untuk Membersihkan Dengan Jumlah Ganjil Dan Menggunakan Jumlah Batu Yang Ganjil

Seseorang harus bersuci tidak kurang dari tiga kali, sebagaimana dalam hadith Salmaan yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya, Nabi #¥ bersabda,

"Janganlah seorang pun di antara kalian bersuci dengan kurang dari tiga batu." Meskipun kesucian penuh tercapai dengan kurang dari tiga usapan, seseorang harus mengusap lagi untuk mencapai jumlah minimal tersebut. Dan jika kesucian penuh tercapai setelah usapan ketiga, dan jika tercapai pada jumlah genap - seperti empat atau enam - maka dianjurkan untuk mengusap satu kali lagi, agar berakhir pada jumlah

ganjil. Dalam hadith yang diriwayatkan oleh Abu Hurairah , Nabi # bersabda,

"| Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree dalam Al-Manaaqib (3860).

"Apabila salah seorang di antara kalian melakukan Al-Istijmaar, maka hendaklah ia melakukannya dengan jumlah ganjil..."""1

12) Ketika Seseorang Berada Di Kamar Mandi, Makruh Baginya Untuk Berbicara

") Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (161), Muslim (237), Ahmad (7180), An-Nassaaee (88), Abu Daawood (35), Ibn Maajah (409), Maalik (34), dan Ad-Daarimee (703). Dan lafaz yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Muslim tentang hadith tersebut.

Bab - 11 Adab Menghadiri Masjid 247

Adab Menghadiri Masjid

"Wahai anak cucu Adam! Pakailah pakaianmu yang indah di setiap (memasuki) masjid, makan dan minumlah, tetapi jangan berlebihan." (Quran 7:31)

Nabi #% bersabda, shal ¢ Zk alan Jy hs te ib J Ol fb tll SG

"Barangsiapa berwudhu untuk salah, lalu menyempurnakannya, kemudian berjalan menuju salah yang diwajibkan, lalu melaksanakannya bersama orang-orang, atau bersama jamaah, atau di masjid - Allah mengampuni dosa-dosanya.""

Adab Menghadiri Masjid

1) Larangan Pergi Ke Masjid Setelah Memakan Bawang Putih [Mentah], Bawang Merah, Atau Apapun Yang Mengeluarkan Bau Tidak Sedap

Ketika seseorang memakan bawang merah atau bawang putih mentah, ia harus menahan diri dari pergi ke masjid, agar tidak menyakiti jamaah lain dengan bau yang tidak sedap; dan barangsiapa menyakiti jamaah maka ia pun menyakiti para malaikat. Jaabir 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda,

" Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim (232).

"Barangsiapa memakan bawang putih atau bawang merah, maka hendaklah ia menjauh dari kami," atau beliau #8 bersabda, 'Maka hendaklah ia menjauh dari masjid kami, dan hendaklah ia duduk di rumahnya.'

Dalam hadith lain, Jaabir # berkata, "Rasulullah # melarang kami memakan bawang merah dan bawang prei, tetapi kami dilanda kebutuhan, sehingga kami pun memakannya. Kemudian beliau # bersabda,

"Barangsiapa memakan dari pohon berbau tidak sedap ini, maka janganlah ia mendekati masjid kami, karena apapun yang menyakiti manusia, para malaikat pun turut tersiksa olehnya."

Meskipun hadith-hadith ini secara tegas melarang kaum muslimin pergi ke masjid setelah memakan bawang putih dan bawang merah, dan meskipun mereka dibebaskan dari dosa karena tidak menghadiri salah berjamaah, namun masih ada orang-orang yang melanggar larangan ini. Allah 4¢ berfirman dalam Kitab-Nya:

"Maka hendaklah orang-orang yang menyalahi perintah Rasul-Nya (yakni Sunnah beliau - cara-cara yang sah, perintah, ibadah, pernyataan, dll.) berhati-hati, jangan sampai mereka ditimpa cobaan (kekufuran, fitnah, bencana, gempa bumi, pembunuhan, dikuasai oleh penguasa yang zalim,

dan sebagainya) atau ditimpa azab yang pedih."

Memang benar, sebagian dari mereka tidak secara sengaja menentang perintah Rasulullah; mereka hanya tidak ingin melewatkan salah berjamaah, namun itu bukanlah alasan yang dapat diterima. Sebagian kaum muslimin dari kalangan awam mengetahui larangan ini tetapi tidak mengindahkannya, suatu kelalaian yang bersumber dari lemahnya iman dalam hati mereka.

i Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (855).

'2] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (854), Muslim (564), Ahmad (14596), An-Nassaaee (707), At-Tirmidhee (1806), dan Abu Daawood (3823). Dan lafaz yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Muslim tentang hadith tersebut.

Bab - 11 Adab Menghadiri Masjid 249

Masalah Terkait: Hukum untuk bawang putih, bawang merah, dan bawang prei berlaku pula untuk segala sesuatu yang berbau tidak sedap dan akan menyakiti jamaah - seperti asap rokok atau bau tidak sedap yang keluar dari tubuh atau pakaian seseorang. Seorang jamaah hendaknya mempertimbangkan kondisinya sebelum pergi ke masjid, agar ia tidak akhirnya berbuat dosa akibat menyakiti kaum muslimin.

Jika Anda memakan bawang putih dan bawang merah kemudian menggunakan sesuatu untuk menghilangkan bau tidak sedap tersebut, Anda boleh pergi ke masjid, namun Anda harus terlebih dahulu memastikan bahwa baunya telah benar-benar hilang dan bau yang keluar dari mulut Anda tidak akan mengganggu jamaah lain. Sebagian orang keliru menggunakan pasta gigi untuk menghilangkan bau bawang putih dan bawang merah, padahal mereka salah, karena bau bawang putih dan bawang merah berasal dari perut dan bukan dari mulut.

2) Dianjurkan Untuk Pergi Ke Masjid Lebih Awal

Nabi #% menganjurkan kaum muslimin untuk pergi ke masjid lebih awal. Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda,

"Seandainya orang-orang mengetahui apa yang terdapat dalam Nidaa (seruan untuk salah, yakni Adzan) dan shaf pertama, dan seandainya mereka tidak menemukan cara lain (untuk berada di shaf pertama dan melakukan azan) kecuali dengan undian, niscaya mereka akan berundi (begitu besarnya keinginan mereka). Dan seandainya orang-orang mengetahui apa yang terdapat dalam (yakni, apa keutamaan) datang lebih awal (ke masjid untuk salah), niscaya mereka akan berlomba-lomba untuknya. Dan seandainya mereka mengetahui apa yang terdapat dalam salah 'Isya dan

Subuh (Fajr) [berjamaah], niscaya mereka akan mendatanginya, sekalipun harus merangkak untuk sampai ke sana,"]

Muslim meriwayatkannya demikian: 'Seandainya kalian mengetahui atau seandainya mereka mengetahui

{1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (615), Muslim (437), Ahmad (7185), At-Tirmidhee (225), An-Nassaaee (540), dan Maalik (151).

apa yang terdapat dalam shaf terdepan, [untuk menentukan siapa yang berada di shaf itu] haruslah diselesaikan dengan undian (karena semua orang akan berlomba untuk berada di shaf itu). Riwayat-riwayat ini dengan jelas menunjukkan keutamaan dan keistimewaan serta pahala yang besar dari datang lebih awal ke masjid. Bahwa Nabi 2% tidak merinci pahala datang lebih awal ke masjid, melainkan memberitahu kita sejauh mana kita akan berusaha seandainya kita mengetahui pahala pastinya, membuktikan bahwa itu sungguh merupakan pahala yang besar.

3) Berjalan Ke Masjid Dengan Tenang Dan Khidmat

Ketika Anda berjalan ke masjid untuk salah, hendaknya Anda tidak berjalan seolah tergesa-gesa atau gelisah; sebaliknya, Anda hendaknya berjalan dengan rasa tenang dan damai. Dengan demikian, Anda lebih mungkin merasa tenteram dan khusyuk dalam salah Anda. Namun jika Anda datang untuk salah dengan tergesa-gesa, saat Anda memasuki keadaan salah, pikiran Anda kemungkinan akan terganggu dan tidak fokus. Nabi # melarang umatnya untuk berlari dan tergesa-gesa menuju salah, bahkan jika salah sedang berlangsung. Abu Qataadah 4 berkata, 'Ketika kami sedang salah bersama Nabi 2%, beliau #% mendengar keributan orang-orang. Ketika beliau selesai salah, beliau # bersabda,

oe SAS tan 'Ada apa dengan kalian?' Mereka berkata, 'Kami bergegas untuk [mengikuti] salah.' Beliau # bersabda, "Jangan lakukan itu. Apabila kalian datang untuk salah, tetaplah menjaga As-Sakeenah (ketenangan dan kekhidmatan). Apapun [bagian dari salah] yang kalian dapatkan,

maka laksanakanlah [itu], dan apapun yang kalian lewatkan, sempurnakanlah (setelah Imam mengucapkan Tasleem).'"?!

] Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (615), Muslim (437, 439), Ahmad (7680), AtTirmidhee (225), dan An-Nassaaee (540).

Bab - 11 Adab Menghadiri Masjid 251

Dan Abu Hurairah 4 meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah # bersabda, "Apabila salah telah diiqamati (ketika Iqamah dikumandangkan dan salah dimulai), maka janganlah kalian berlari (berlari atau jogging) menuju salah tersebut, melainkan datanglah dengan berjalan, dengan membawa rasa As-Sakeenah (ketenangan dan keheningan) pada diri kalian. Apapun yang kalian dapatkan, maka laksanakanlah; dan apapun yang kalian lewatkan, maka sempurnakanlah (setelah Imam mengucapkan Tasleem)."")

Seseorang yang merenungkan kedua hadith ini mungkin akan memperhatikan perbedaan di antara keduanya: riwayat Abu Qataadah menggunakan redaksi, "Ketika kamu datang ke salah," sementara riwayat Abu Hurairah menggunakan redaksi, "Ketika salah Ugeemat (ketika Iqaamah dikumandangkan dan salah dimulai)." Apakah ada perbedaan makna antara kedua redaksi tersebut? Menurut riwayat Abu Qataadah, ketika seorang jamaah pergi ke masjid, ia harus berjalan dengan penuh ketenangan dan tidak tergesa-gesa, baik salah telah dimulai maupun belum. Riwayat Abu Hurairah, di sisi lain, menunjukkan alasan utama mengapa orang berlari ke masjid: salah sedang berlangsung, dan mereka tidak ingin melewatkan satu pun bagiannya. Itulah makna gabungan dari kedua redaksi tersebut, dan Allah 3 lebih mengetahui.

4) Du'a yang Hendaknya Kamu Baca Ketika Berjalan Menuju Masjid untuk Salah

Ketika kamu berjalan ke masjid, dianjurkan bagimu untuk mengucapkan

apa yang diucapkan Nabi #% ketika beliau keluar untuk salah. Ibn

'Abbaas 4 meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi #%, 'Kemudian keluar untuk

salah, dan beliau # sedang mengucapkan,
content_ar: الباب - 10 The الآداب Of Going To The Bathroom 245 أبو هريرة later related, ‘Then I said, ‘What is the problem with bones and manure?’ He ## said, ‘They are the food of Jinns.’ A delegation of Jinns from Naseebeen - and الخير indeed are those Jinns - came to me and asked me for provision, and لذا I invoked الله 8% for them - that they should never pass by any bones or manure except that they should find food upon them.”“) This narration makes clear the العقل why يُحرَّم to purify oneself with either bones or manure. Related Issue: Based on the above-mentioned ruling for the food of الجن, it is أيضاً forbidden to perform Al-Istinjaa or Al-Istijmaar with the food of human beings. Similarly, يُحرَّم to perform Al-Istinjaa or Al-Istijmaar with honored paper, مثل paper on which religious العلم is written, for that paper is likely to have written on it الآيات of the القرآن الكريم or الله’s beautiful Names and Attributes. And an even worst offence is to use the Qur’an itself. 11) عندما One Performs Al-Istijmaar, It ls Recommended To Clean An Odd Number Of Times And To Use An Odd Number Of Stones ينبغي للمرء purify himself no less than three times, for in the previously related الحديث of Salmaan , النبي ﷺ #¥ said, “Let no one from you purify himself with less than three stones.” Even إذا full purification is achieved with less than three wipes, على المرء wipe again لتحقيق the minimum amount. And إذا ull purification is achieved بعد the ثالثاً wiping, and إذا it is ichieved at an even number - مثل four or six - then it is ‘ecommended to wipe one more time, من أجل finish at an odd wmber. In a الحديث related by أبو هريرة , النبي ﷺ # aid, "| Related by Bukhaaree in Al-Manaaqib (3860). “عندما one of you performs Al-Istijmaar, then let him perform it an odd number of times...”""1 12) عندما One Is In The Bathroom, يُكره For Him To Talk ") Related by Bukhaaree (161), مسلم (237), أحمد (7180), An-Nassaaee (88), Abu Daawood (35), Ibn Maajah (409), Maalik (34), and Ad-Daarimee (703). And the above-mentioned wording is from مسلم’s narration of the الحديث. الباب - 11 The الآداب Of Attending Masjids 247 The الآداب Of Attending Masjids “O الأولاد of آدم! Take your adornment (by wearing your clean clothes), بينما praying and going round (the Tawaf of) the Ka‘bah.” (القرآن الكريم 7:31) النبي ﷺ #% said, shal ¢ Zk alan Jy hs te ib J Ol fb tll SG “Whoever performs ablution (الوضوء) for الصلاة, performs it well, then walks to the prescribed الصلاة, and then prays it with the people, or with the congregation, or in the المسجد - الله forgives for him his الذنوب.”" The Etiquettes Of Attending The المسجد 1) The Prohibition Of Going To The المسجد بعد Eating [Raw] Garlic, Onion, Or Anything Else That Emits A Foul Smell عندما a person eats raw onion or garlic, he should abstain from going to the المسجد, لذا as to avoid inflicting harm upon other worshippers with his foul smell; and whosoever harms worshippers harms the الملائكة as well. Jaabir 4 reported that النبي ﷺ # said, " Related by مسلم (232). “Whoever eats garlic or onion, then let him withdraw from us,” or he #8 said, ‘Then let him stay away from our المسجد, and let him sit in his home.’ In another الحديث, Jaabir # said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله # forbade us from eating onion and leek, but we became overcome by need, and لذا we ate from them. Then he # said, “Whoever eats from this foul-smelling tree, then let him not come near our المسجد, for whatever man is harmed by, الملائكة are harmed by as well." حتى وإن these Ahadeeth expressly prohibit المسلمون from going to the المسجد بعد eating garlic and onion, and حتى وإن they are absolved of الذنب for not attending congregational الصلاة, some people still violate the prohibition. الله 4¢ said in His الكتاب: “And let those who oppose الرسول ﷺ’s (محمد ﷺ 3%) commandment (i.e., his السنة - legal ways, orders, acts of worship, statements, etc.) beware, lest some Fitnah (disbelief, الابتلاءات, afflictions, earthquakes, killing, overpowered by a tyrant, etc.) befall them or a painful torment be inflicted on them.” True, some of them do not intentionally oppose الرسول ﷺ’s commandment; they simply don’t want to miss the congregational الصلاة, yet that is not an acceptable excuse. Some المسلمون from among the masses know about the prohibition but pay no heed to it, an act of negligence that stems from weak Eemaan in their القلوب. i Related by Bukhaaree (855). '2] Related by Bukhaaree (854), مسلم (564), أحمد (14596), An-Nassaaee (707), At-Tirmidhee (1806), and Abu Daawood (3823). And the abovementioned wording is from مسلم’s narration of the الحديث, الباب - 11 The الآداب Of Attending Masjids 249 Related Issue: The ruling for garlic, onion, and leek applies to anything that has a foul odor and that will inflict harm upon worshippers - مثل cigarette smoke or foul odors that emanate from a person’s الجسد or clothes. A worshipper should consider his situation قبل he goes to the المسجد, لذا that he doesn’t end up sinning ونتيجة لذلك of inflicting harm on المسلمون. إذا you eat garlic and onion and then use something to suppress their foul odor, you may go to the المسجد, but you must أولاً be sure that the smell is completely suppressed and that the smell coming from your mouth will not bother other worshippers. Some people make the mistake of using toothpaste to get rid of the smell of garlic and onion, but they are الخطأ, for the smell of garlic and onion originates from the stomach and not from the mouth. 2) يستحب To Go To The المسجد Early النبي ﷺ #% exhorted المسلمون to go to the المسجد early. أبو هريرة # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “Had people known what is in the Nidaa (the calling to the الصلاة, ie., the Adhaan) and the أولاً row, and had they found no الطريق (to be in the أولاً row and to perform the call to الصلاة) except by casting lots, they would have cast lots (لذا determined would they be). And had people known what is in (i.e., what the الفضائل are) being early (to the المسجد for الصلاة), they would have raced to it. And had they known what is in the [congregational] ‘Eesha and Morning (Fajr) الصلوات, they would have come to them, even إذا they had to crawl من أجل get there,"] مسلم narrated it وهكذا: ‘Had you known or had they known {1 Related by Bukhaaree (615), مسلم (437), أحمد (7185), At-Tirmidhee (225), An-Nassaaee (540), and Maalik (151). what is in the front row, [seeing who would be in that row] would have had to be settled by casting lots (لأن everyone would race to be in that row). These narrations clearly show the الفضيلة and superiority and great الأجر of going early to the المسجد. That النبي ﷺ 2% did not specify the الأجر for going to the المسجد early, but instead informed us what lengths we would go to had we known the exact الأجر, proves that it is indeed a great الأجر. 3) Walking To The المسجد With Calmness And Serenity عندما you walk to the المسجد for الصلاة, you should not walk as إذا you are rushed or agitated; instead, you should walk with a sense of calmness and peace. ونتيجة لذلك, you are more likely to feel peaceful and concentrated in your الصلاة. But إذا you come to الصلاة in a hurry, by the time you enter into its state, your العقل is likely to be distracted and unfocused. النبي ﷺ # forbade the people of his Nation from running and rushing to the الصلاة, even إذا the الصلاة is in progress. Abu Qataadah 4 said, ‘بينما we were praying with النبي ﷺ 2%, he #% heard the clamor of men. عندما he finished praying, he # said, oe SAS tan ‘What is the matter with you?’ قالوا, ‘We hurried to [join the] الصلاة.’ He # said, “Do not do لذا. عندما you come to الصلاة, adhere to As-Sakeenah (calmness and quietness). Whatever [part of the الصلاة] you get, then pray [it], and whatever you miss, complete it (بعد the الإمام makes Tasleem).’"?! ] Related by Bukhaaree (615), مسلم (437, 439), أحمد (7680), AtTirmidhee (225), and An-Nassaaee (540). الباب - 11 The الآداب Of Attending Masjids 251 And أبو هريرة 4 reported that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله # say, “عندما the الصلاة Ugeemat (عندما the Iqaamah is made and the الصلاة commences), then do not come racing (running or jogging) to it, but instead come to it, walking, with a sense of As-Sakeenah (peace and quite) about you. Whatever you get, then pray it; and whatever you miss, then complete it (بعد the الإمام makes Tasleem).”") One who reflects on both Ahadeeth might notice a difference between them: Abu Qataadah’s narration has the wording, “عندما you come to الصلاة,” بينما أبو هريرة’s narration has the wording, “عندما the الصلاة Ugeemat (عندما the Iqaamah is made and the الصلاة commences).” Is there a difference in meaning between the two wordings? وفقاً لـ Abu Qataadah’s narration, عندما a worshipper goes to the المسجد, he must go with a sense of calm and quiet about him, regardless of whether the الصلاة has commenced or not. أبو هريرة’s narration, من ناحية أخرى, points to the الرئيسي العقل why people run to the المسجد: the الصلاة is in progress, and they don’t want to miss any of it. That is the combined meaning of both wordings, and الله 3 knows best. 4) What الدعاء You Should Say بينما You Are Walking To The المسجد For الصلاة عندما you walk to the المسجد, يستحب for you to say what النبي ﷺ #% said عندما he would go out for الصلاة. Ibn ‘Abbaas 4 related that النبي ﷺ #%, ‘Then went out for الصلاة, and he # was saying,
